survey.name: Survey
survey.res: You have left question(s) unanswered. Please provide an answer.
You have left question(s) unanswered. Please provide an answer.
You have left some questions unanswered. You will find the items that need you attention shown below.

res.label: PROMPTERROR
res.strong: You have left question(s) unanswered. Are you sure you do not have an opinion? If not, you can skip to the next question.

res.label: PROMPTERROR1
res.strong: You have left question(s) unanswered. Are you sure you do not have an opinion? If not, you can skip to the next question.

res.label: PROMPTERROR2
res.strong: You have left question(s) unanswered. Are you sure you do not have an opinion? If not, you can skip to the next question.

samplesource.title: Dynata
samplesource.invalid: You are missing information in the URL. Please verify the URL with the original invite.
samplesource.completed: It seems you have already entered this survey.

samplesource.title: Open Survey
samplesource.invalid: You are missing information in the URL. Please verify the URL with the original invite.
samplesource.completed: It seems you have already entered this survey.
samplesource.exit: Thank you for taking our survey.
Thank you for taking our survey. Your efforts are greatly appreciated!
Thank you for taking our survey.

survey.exec: p.maxdiff1=[]

radio.label: Mode
radio.title: Mode

radio.label: MC
radio.title: MC

radio.label: XMOBILE
radio.title: XMOBILE (COMPUTED)

survey.exec: p.pri1=0

checkbox.label: Flag_prism
checkbox.title: Survey completed or not
checkbox.exec:
s = str(comp_sflag) b="123456789a" letters = [x for x in b] # 1 = not complete the survey, 0 = Already completed the survey
flags = [0 if ch in s else 1 for ch in letters]
for row, flag in zip(Flag_prism.rows, flags): if flag: row.val = 1

p.span: Thank you for taking time to complete this important survey. Your voice contributes to a broader effort to understand how individuals make sense of health care and public policy. Insights from this study help inform conversations about the future of health care in the United States.

survey.exec: vscreenout.val= str('QS1') setMarker('Screened-QS1')

text.label: QZIP
text.title: Please enter your 5-digit ZIP Code (the Zip Code of your primary residence where you are registered to vote)

survey.exec: ZIPCODES1 = zipCodeFile("find_duplicate.dat", "unique") ZIPCODES2 = zipCodeFile("find_duplicate.dat", "d2") ZIPCODES3 = zipCodeFile("find_duplicate.dat", "d3") ZIPCODES4 = zipCodeFile("find_duplicate.dat", "d4") ZIPCODES5 = zipCodeFile("find_duplicate.dat", "d5") myzip=zipCodeFile("Zipcodefile.dat", "ZIP")

radio.label: xzip_duplicate
radio.title: Hidden: Maxdiff selection

radio.label: xzip_count
radio.title: Hidden: Maxdiff selection

radio.label: XQREGION
radio.title: ${this.label}. CENSUS REGION
radio.exec:
myval=str(QZIP.val) myval=myval+str(xzip_count.rows[xzip_count.val].text) respinfo=myzip.get(myval)
if respinfo and respinfo['region']!="": XQREGION.val=XQREGION.attr("r"+str(respinfo['region'])).index else: XQREGION.val=XQREGION.r99.index

radio.label: XQDIVISION
radio.title: ${this.label}. CENSUS DIVISION
radio.exec:
myval=str(QZIP.val) myval=myval+str(xzip_count.rows[xzip_count.val].text) respinfo=myzip.get(myval)
if respinfo and respinfo['division'] != "": XQDIVISION.val=XQDIVISION.attr("r"+str(respinfo['division'])).index else: XQDIVISION.val=XQDIVISION.r99.index

text.label: XQCDNO
text.title: ${this.label}. QCDNO
text.exec:
myval=str(QZIP.val) myval=myval+str(xzip_count.rows[xzip_count.val].text) respinfo=myzip.get(myval)
if respinfo and respinfo['cdno'] != "": XQCDNO.r1.val=respinfo['cdno'] else: XQCDNO.r99.val=1

radio.label: XQSTATE
radio.title: ${this.label}. STATE
radio.exec:
myval=str(QZIP.val) myval=myval+str(xzip_count.rows[xzip_count.val].text) respinfo=myzip.get(myval)
if respinfo and respinfo['state'] != "": XQSTATE.val=XQSTATE.attr(respinfo['state'].upper()).index else: XQSTATE.val=XQSTATE.r99.index

text.label: XQCD
text.title: ${this.label}. CD
text.exec:
myval=str(QZIP.val) myval=myval+str(xzip_count.rows[xzip_count.val].text) respinfo=myzip.get(myval)
if respinfo and respinfo['cd'] != "": XQCD.r1.val=respinfo['cd'] else: XQCD.r99.val=1

text.label: XQUSREP
text.title: ${this.label}. CONGRESSPERSON
text.exec:
myval=str(QZIP.val) myval=myval+str(xzip_count.rows[xzip_count.val].text) respinfo=myzip.get(myval)
if respinfo and respinfo['usrep'] != "": XQUSREP.r1.val=respinfo['usrep'] else: XQUSREP.r99.val=1

text.label: XQSEN1
text.title: ${this.label}. SENATOR 1
text.exec:
myval=str(QZIP.val) myval=myval+str(xzip_count.rows[xzip_count.val].text) respinfo=myzip.get(myval)
if respinfo and respinfo['sen1'] != "": XQSEN1.r1.val=respinfo['sen1'] else: XQSEN1.r99.val=1

text.label: XQSEN2
text.title: ${this.label}. SENATOR 2
text.exec:
myval=str(QZIP.val) myval=myval+str(xzip_count.rows[xzip_count.val].text) respinfo=myzip.get(myval)
if respinfo and (respinfo['sen2'] != "" and respinfo['sen2'] != '0'): XQSEN2.r1.val=respinfo['sen2'] else: XQSEN2.r99.val=1

radio.label: XQPrimaryRUCA
radio.title: ${this.label}. PrimaryRUCA
radio.exec:
myval=str(QZIP.val) myval=myval+str(xzip_count.rows[xzip_count.val].text) respinfo=myzip.get(myval)
if respinfo and respinfo['primaryruca'] != "": XQPrimaryRUCA.val=XQPrimaryRUCA.attr("r"+str(respinfo['primaryruca'])).index else: XQPrimaryRUCA.val=XQPrimaryRUCA.r99.index

radio.label: XQSecondaryRUCA
radio.title: ${this.label}. SecondaryRUCA
radio.exec:
myval=str(QZIP.val) myval=myval+str(xzip_count.rows[xzip_count.val].text) respinfo=myzip.get(myval)
if respinfo and respinfo['secondaryruca'] != "": XQSecondaryRUCA.val=XQSecondaryRUCA.attr("r"+str(respinfo['secondaryruca'])).index else: XQSecondaryRUCA.val=XQSecondaryRUCA.r99.index

radio.label: QAGECAT5
radio.title: AGE GROUP (HIDDEN)

radio.label: QGENDER
radio.title: ${this.label} Which of the following best describes your gender?

radio.label: QVOTE24

title.strong: Who did you vote for in the latest (2024) Presidential election?

radio.row: I did not have the chance to vote

survey.exec: vscreenout.val= str('QVOTE24') setMarker('Screened-QVOTE24')

radio.label: QS5
radio.title: ${this.label} In politics today, do you consider yourself a…?

title.strong: Would you say you are a strong or not-so strong [pipe: QS5]?
Do you lean towards the Republican or Democratic Party?

survey.exec: vscreenout.val= str('QS5') setMarker('Screened-QS5')
survey.term: Voted for someone else and is Independent

radio.label: TYPING_MODULE
radio.title: Final punch of the segmentation:

radio.label: POLICY
radio.title: Below are policy changes some have proposed. For each, please indicate how much you support or oppose the idea

strong.span: The government should create a public insurance option to compete with private plans

span.strong: Health insurance should be provided through a single national health insurance system run by the government.
Large employers should be required to provide health insurance to their workers.

radio.label: ATTITUDE
radio.title: For each of the following statements, indicate the extent to which you agree or disagree…

span.strong: The federal government should invest extra funds in communities of color that have suffered historic health injustices, even if it means higher taxes
Big corporations have too much influence over public health policies
Mandating any medical treatment violates personal liberty

radio.label: IDEOLOGY
radio.title: Indicate which statement comes closest to your own view:

row.label: r1
row.rightLegend: We need to focus on America first

span.strong: America is better off as a leader in the global economy

row.label: r2
row.rightLegend: The private, free market healthcare system is better because it protects patient choice and access to higher quality care

span.strong: Healthcare is a basic right and government should be responsible for providing healthcare to all Americans

radio.label: TRUST
radio.title: Rate how much you trust each of the following…

html.label: Maxdiff_Intro
html.p: In this section, you’ll see a series of short statements expressing different points of view. Each statement reflects a way of thinking about issues people often care deeply about including health, healthcare, community and politics.
html.ul:
The statement that sounds MOST like your own point of view, and
The statement that sounds LEAST like your own point of view.

p.em: Some statements may appear again later. That’s intentional — it helps us understand how people think across different kinds of ideas.

html.p: There are no right or wrong answers. Simply go with your first reaction each time.

text.label: XPOLICY
text.title: HIDDEN: POLICY
text.row: The government should create a public insurance option to compete with private plans
Health insurance should be provided through a single national health insurance system run by the government.
Large employers should be required to provide health insurance to their workers.

text.label: XIDEOLOGY
text.title: HIDDEN: IDEOLOGY
text.exec:
IDEOLOGY_U = [3.5363, 4.4206] IDEOLOGY_S = [2.04099, 2.02961] IDEOLOGY_F = [float(x+1) for x in IDEOLOGY.values] IDEOLOGY_AVG = []
XIDEOLOGY.r1.val = ((IDEOLOGY_F[0] - IDEOLOGY_U[0])/IDEOLOGY_S[0])*-1 XIDEOLOGY.r2.val = (IDEOLOGY_F[1] - IDEOLOGY_U[1])/IDEOLOGY_S[1]
XDEM_SEG.r12.c1.val = IDEOLOGY_F XDEM_SEG.r12.c2.val = IDEOLOGY_U XDEM_SEG.r12.c3.val = IDEOLOGY_S XDEM_SEG.r12.c4.val = XIDEOLOGY.r1.val

text.label: XATTITUDE
text.title: HIDDEN: ATTITUDE
text.exec:
ATTITUDE_U = [5.009, 5.631, 5.2571] ATTITUDE_S = [1.28372, 1.02132, 1.14707] ATTITUDE_F = [float(x+1) for x in ATTITUDE.values]
for i in range(3): XATTITUDE.rows[i].val = (ATTITUDE_F[i] - ATTITUDE_U[i])/ATTITUDE_S[i]
XDEM_SEG.r13.c1.val = ATTITUDE_F[0] XDEM_SEG.r14.c1.val = ATTITUDE_F[1]
XDEM_SEG.r13.c2.val = ATTITUDE_U XDEM_SEG.r14.c2.val = ATTITUDE_U
XDEM_SEG.r13.c3.val = ATTITUDE_S XDEM_SEG.r14.c3.val = ATTITUDE_S
XDEM_SEG.r13.c4.val = XATTITUDE.r1.val XDEM_SEG.r14.c4.val = XATTITUDE.r2.val
text.row: The government should create a public insurance option to compete with private plans
Health insurance should be provided through a single national health insurance system run by the government.
Large employers should be required to provide health insurance to their workers.

text.label: XTRUST
text.title: HIDDEN: TRUST
text.exec:
TRUST_U = [3.2642, 4.5526, 4.6011, 3.6061, 3.9617] TRUST_S = [1.68737, 1.50961, 1.53148, 1.5441 , 1.49655] TRUST_F = [float(x+1) for x in TRUST.values]
print TRUST_F for i in range(5): XTRUST.rows[i].val = (TRUST_F[i] - TRUST_U[i])/TRUST_S[i]

block.label: DEMBlock
block.builder:title: DEM Block

number.label: QMD1_Version
number.title:
QMD1 - MaxDiff Version
Pick your version for testing... will be automated when live...

radio.label: QDEM_[loopvar: task]
radio.title: Select the one statement that sounds MOST like your own point of view (on the left), and the statement that sounds LEAST like you (on the right)
radio.exec: setupMaxDiffItemsI( QMD1_md, "v%d_t%d" % (QMD1_Version.val, [loopvar: task]), QDEM_[loopvar: task]) p.MDcount = str(QMD1_md_loop_expanded.order.index([loopvar: task]-1)+1)
radio.row: Tech and data can improve care, but only if we stop bias, protect privacy, and provide benefits fairly.
I trust mainstream experts and prefer step-by-step fixes—strengthening the ACA, not ripping it up.
Digital tools, AI, and start-ups can universalize care faster than big bureaucracies—let’s remove red tape.
Corporate greed hurts ordinary people. Government must crack down on prices, ads, and junk food.
Healthcare is a human right. We need Medicare-for-All or something very close to it.
Unions fought for good coverage. Policy should protect employer benefits and bargaining power.
Racism and inequality drive poor health. Equity must be built into every health decision and budget.
My faith calls me to fight for the poor and for broader access—especially in under-served communities.
Climate change is a public-health emergency. Clean energy and environmental justice are health policy.

text.label: QMD1
text.title: Hidden: QDEM
text.exec:
p.sum=[] print p.maxdiff1
groupSize = 4
# split into chunks of 4 groups = [p.maxdiff1[i:i+groupSize] for i in range(0, len(p.maxdiff1), groupSize)]
print(groups) for col in range(0, 10): rowsForColumn = groups[col]
for r in rowsForColumn: rowNum = int(r)-1 print rowNum,col QMD1.rows[rowNum][col].val=0
for i in range(0,10): best = "QDEM_{}".format(i+1) worst = "QDEM_{}".format(i+1) best1=allQuestions[best].best.val worst1=allQuestions[worst].worst.val QMD1.rows[best1][i].val=1 QMD1.rows[worst1][i].val=-1
for i in QMD1.rows: a=0 for j in range(10): if QMD1[i][j].val!=None: a1=QMD1[i][j].val a+=int(a1) QMD1[i].c11.val=a

text.label: XDEM_SEG
text.title: HIDDEN: XDEM_SEG
text.row: Tech and data can improve care, but only if we stop bias, protect privacy, and provide benefits fairly.
I trust mainstream experts and prefer step-by-step fixes—strengthening the ACA, not ripping it up.
Digital tools, AI, and start-ups can universalize care faster than big bureaucracies—let’s remove red tape.
Corporate greed hurts ordinary people. Government must crack down on prices, ads, and junk food.
Healthcare is a human right. We need Medicare-for-All or something very close to it.
Unions fought for good coverage. Policy should protect employer benefits and bargaining power.
Racism and inequality drive poor health. Equity must be built into every health decision and budget.
My faith calls me to fight for the poor and for broader access—especially in under-served communities.
Climate change is a public-health emergency. Clean energy and environmental justice are health policy.

radio.label: XDEM_SEG_FINAL_1
radio.title: CENTROID MODEL - DEMOCRAT SEGMENTS - First Minimum

radio.label: XDEM_SEG_FINAL_2
radio.title: CENTROID MODEL - DEMOCRAT SEGMENTS - Second Minimum

text.label: QSEG_3
text.title: Hidden: QDEM
text.row: Tech and data can improve care, but only if we stop bias, protect privacy, and provide benefits fairly.
I trust mainstream experts and prefer step-by-step fixes—strengthening the ACA, not ripping it up.
Digital tools, AI, and start-ups can universalize care faster than big bureaucracies—let’s remove red tape.
Corporate greed hurts ordinary people. Government must crack down on prices, ads, and junk food.
Healthcare is a human right. We need Medicare-for-All or something very close to it.
Unions fought for good coverage. Policy should protect employer benefits and bargaining power.
Racism and inequality drive poor health. Equity must be built into every health decision and budget.
Reproductive freedom—including abortion access—is non-negotiable and central to health policy.
My faith calls me to fight for the poor and for broader access—especially in under-served communities.

text.label: XQSEG_3_EXP
text.title: Hidden: QDEM

radio.label: XDEM1_SEG_FINAL_1
radio.title: LOGIT MODEL - DEMOCRAT SEGMENTS - First Max

radio.label: XDEM1_SEG_FINAL_2
radio.title: LOGIT MODEL - DEMOCRAT SEGMENTS - Second Max

block.label: b12
block.builder:title: GOP Block

number.label: QMD3_Version
number.title:
QMD1 - MaxDiff Version
Pick your version for testing... will be automated when live...

radio.label: QGOP_[loopvar: task]
radio.title: Select the one statement that sounds MOST like your own point of view (on the left), and the statement that sounds LEAST like you (on the right)
radio.exec: setupMaxDiffItemsI( QMD3_md, "v%d_t%d" % (QMD3_Version.val, [loopvar: task]), QGOP_[loopvar: task]) p.MDcount = str(QMD3_md_loop_expanded.order.index([loopvar: task]-1)+1)
radio.row: Government should stay small but defend marriage, family life, and personal responsibility.
Big pharma and big agribusiness can’t be trusted; local food and local control are healthier.
Drug and hospital prices are crushing people. I don’t care who fixes it—just bring costs down.
Small-town hospitals keep closing. Government must improve access to care for rural America.
Vaccines are unsafe, full stop. No one should be forced or pressured to take them.
Modern medicine ignores the whole person. Natural and alternative therapies deserve equal respect and coverage.
Clean, whole-food living—not pills—keeps us healthy. The system ignores it because there’s no profit in it.
Human beings thrive on meat-heavy, low-carb diets. Government guidelines are junk science.
We must protect U.S. patients, workers, and supply chains first. Foreign competition and immigration come second.
Free markets work best, but I defer to FDA and CDC experts on safety and innovation.
Competition—private insurance, transparent prices, fewer rules—is the key to cheaper, better care.

text.label: QMD3
text.title: Hidden: QDEM
text.exec:
p.sum=[] print p.maxdiff1
groupSize = 4
# split into chunks of 4 groups = [p.maxdiff1[i:i+groupSize] for i in range(0, len(p.maxdiff1), groupSize)]
print(groups) for col in range(0, 12): rowsForColumn = groups[col]
for r in rowsForColumn: rowNum = int(r)-1 print rowNum,col QMD3.rows[rowNum][col].val=0
for i in range(0,12): best = "QGOP_{}".format(i+1) worst = "QGOP_{}".format(i+1) best1=allQuestions[best].best.val worst1=allQuestions[worst].worst.val QMD3.rows[best1][i].val=1 QMD3.rows[worst1][i].val=-1
for i in QMD3.rows: a=0 for j in range(12): if QMD3[i][j].val!=None: a1=QMD3[i][j].val a+=int(a1) QMD3[i].c13.val=a
text.row: Government should stay small but defend marriage, family life, and personal responsibility.
Big pharma and big agribusiness can’t be trusted; local food and local control are healthier
Big pharma and big agribusiness can’t be trusted; local food and local control are healthier
Small-town hospitals keep closing. Government must improve access to care for rural America
Vaccines are unsafe, full stop. No one should be forced or pressured to take them
My body, my choice—whether mandates, supplements, or experimental drugs. Government should butt out
Modern medicine ignores the whole person. Natural and alternative therapies deserve equal respect and coverage
Clean, whole-food living—not pills—keeps us healthy. The system ignores it because there’s no profit in it
Human beings thrive on meat-heavy, low-carb diets. Government guidelines are junk science
We must protect U.S. patients, workers, and supply chains first. Foreign competition and immigration come second
Free markets work best, but I defer to FDA and CDC experts on safety and innovation
Competition—private insurance, transparent prices, fewer rules—is the key to cheaper, better care

text.label: XGOP_SEG
text.title: HIDDEN: XGOP_SEG
text.row: Government should stay small but defend marriage, family life, and personal responsibility.
Big pharma and big agribusiness can’t be trusted; local food and local control are healthier.
Drug and hospital prices are crushing people. I don’t care who fixes it—just bring costs down.
Small-town hospitals keep closing. Government must improve access to care for rural America.
Vaccines are unsafe, full stop. No one should be forced or pressured to take them.
My body, my choice—whether mandates, supplements, or experimental drugs. Government should butt out.
Modern medicine ignores the whole person. Natural and alternative therapies deserve equal respect and coverage.
Clean, whole-food living—not pills—keeps us healthy. The system ignores it because there’s no profit in it.
Human beings thrive on meat-heavy, low-carb diets. Government guidelines are junk science.
We must protect U.S. patients, workers, and supply chains first. Foreign competition and immigration come second.
Free markets work best, but I defer to FDA and CDC experts on safety and innovation.
Competition—private insurance, transparent prices, fewer rules—is the key to cheaper, better care.

radio.label: XGOP_SEG_FINAL_1
radio.title: CENTROID MODEL - REPUBLICAN SEGMENTS - First minimum

radio.label: XGOP_SEG_FINAL_2
radio.title: CENTROID MODEL - REPUBLICAN SEGMENTS - Second minimum

text.label: QSEG_4
text.title: Hidden: QDEM
text.exec: QSEG_4.r1.val=0 QSEG_4.r2.val=(float(XTRUST.r1.val) + float(XTRUST.r2.val) + float(XTRUST.r3.val))/3 QSEG_4.r3.val=(float(XTRUST.r4.val) + float(XTRUST.r5.val))/2 QSEG_4.r4.val=XATTITUDE.r3.val QSEG_4.r5.val=XIDEOLOGY.r2.val

text.label: XQSEG_4_EXP
text.title: Hidden: QDEM

radio.label: XGOP1_SEG_FINAL_1
radio.title: LOGIT MODEL - REPUBLICAN SEGMENTS - First Max

radio.label: XGOP1_SEG_FINAL_2
radio.title: LOGIT MODEL - REPUBLICAN SEGMENTS - Second Max

radio.label: XSEG_ASSIGNED
radio.title: Final Segment Assignment (1-16)

radio.label: XMODEL_AGREEMENT
radio.title: Model Agreement Status

float.label: XCENTROID_DIST1
float.title: Centroid Distance to Closest Segment

float.label: XCENTROID_DIST2
float.title: Centroid Distance to 2nd Closest Segment

float.label: XREL_DIFF
float.title: Relative Difference: (Dist2 - Dist1) / Dist1

float.label: XLOGIT_PROB1
float.title: Logit Probability % for Top Choice

radio.label: XCONF_LEVEL
radio.title: Confidence Level Classification

radio.label: XAMB_FLAG
radio.title: Ambiguity Flag
radio.exec:
no_agreement = (XMODEL_AGREEMENT.r3.val == 1) if XMODEL_AGREEMENT.any else False dist1 = float(XCENTROID_DIST1.val) if XCENTROID_DIST1.val else 99 rel_diff = float(XREL_DIFF.val) if XREL_DIFF.val else 0 logit_prob = float(XLOGIT_PROB1.val) if XLOGIT_PROB1.val else 0
if no_agreement or dist1 >= 0.5 or not (rel_diff > 0.5) or not (logit_prob > 50): XAMB_FLAG.val = XAMB_FLAG.r2.index else: XAMB_FLAG.val = XAMB_FLAG.r1.index

radio.label: QTOT_FOLLOWERS
radio.title: ${this.label} Across all your public social-media accounts (X/Twitter, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook pages, Substack, podcast, YouTube, etc.), about how many followers or subscribers do you have in total?

radio.label: QPREG_ELECTED
radio.title: Have you ever done any of the following?
radio.row: Been a paid staffer or advisor to an elected official or gov’t agency
Held an elected or appointed role in a party committee
Contacted an elected official or their staff
Helped recruit a candidate to run for public office
Helped recruit staff for a political campaign, office of an elected official, or other political appointee
Met (in person or video) with congressional, agency, or state legislative staff
Sat on the board or executive committee of any health, policy, or business association
Worked with or advised a Washington-based organization (think tank, patient advocacy group, trade association)
Testified or submitted written comments to a state legislative or Congressional committee
Spoke at a public meeting, podcast, webinar, church or civic group on a health topic
Wrote an article, blog post, Substack, or op-ed on an issue
Organized an event or rally on any issue

title.span: In the past 12 months, have you taken any of the following actions related to a public policy issue?

radio.label: QPREG_TOT_FOLLOWERS
radio.title: Across all your public social-media accounts (X/Twitter, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook pages, Substack, podcast, YouTube, etc.), about how many followers or subscribers do you have in total?
radio.row: None – I don’t use social media

survey.exec: Punch3(QPREG_TOT_FOLLOWERS)

radio.label: QPREG_SM
radio.title: ${this.label}. Thinking about the past 30 days, across any social media platform you use (Facebook, X, LinkedIn, TikTok, or others): Approximately how many times have you done any of the following to advocate on a public policy or other important issue:
radio.row: Commented or replied on posts about a public policy or other important issue?
Created an original post or video

title.span: Indicate how much you either agree or disagree with the following statements…

strong.span: There are important issues I care about, but don’t always make time to act on.
Sharing my views publicly is just as important as taking concrete steps to change policy.
To confirm you are able to read the screen, please select option below for “Somewhat Disagree”

radio.label: QPREG_SM2_r3_flag
radio.title: Hidden: QPREG_SM2

radio.label: MAHA_AWARE

title.span: How familiar are you with the term ‘MAHA’ (Make America Healthy Again)?

radio.row: Very familiar – I know a lot about it
Somewhat familiar – I know about it
Aware, but not sure what it means
Heard of it, but know nothing about it

radio.label: MAHA_PERCEPTION

title.span: When you hear the term MAHA, what does it most closely represent to you?

radio.row: A movement focused on food quality, nutrition, and chronic disease
A broader critique of drug and food companies, and the health system
A political slogan more than a real movement
A personal health or lifestyle philosophy
A fringe or extreme health movement
The movement around Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.
The health movement led by Donald Trump
Something unclear or hard to define

title.span: Overall, what is your impression of the MAHA movement or idea?

radio.label: MAHA_IDENTITY

title.span: Which best describes your relationship to the MAHA label?

radio.row: I am a member of the national organization
I agree with some of the ideas, but wouldn’t identify with it
I’ve heard of it, but don’t relate to it

radio.label: QD2

title.div:
${this.label} Are you, yourself, of Hispanic or Latino background?
such as Mexican, Puerto Rican, Cuban, or some other Spanish background

radio.row: No – not of Hispanic, Latino or Spanish origin

survey.exec: Punch3(QD2)

radio.label: QD3
radio.title: ${this.label} Which of the following best describes your race?

survey.exec: Punch3(QD3)

radio.label: QRACE_ETHNIC
radio.title: RACE-ETHNICITY (COLLAPSED)

radio.label: QVET
radio.title: ${this.label} Have you ever served on active duty in the U.S. Armed Forces, Reserves, or National Guard?

radio.label: QUNION
radio.title: ${this.label} Are you currently, or have you ever been, part of a labor union?

radio.label: QEDU
radio.title: ${this.label} Please indicate the highest level of school you completed or the highest degree you have received…

radio.label: XEDU_CAT
radio.title: EDUCATION GROUPS

radio.label: QREL
radio.title: ${this.label} What is your present religion, if any?
radio.row: Orthodox, such as Greek or Russian Orthodox

radio.label: QEVANGEL
radio.title: ${this.label} Would you describe yourself a born-again or evangelical Christian, or not?

radio.label: QREL_CAT
radio.title: RELIGIOUS CATEGORIES

radio.label: QD23
radio.title: ${this.label} Last year – that is, in 2024 – what was your total family income from all sources, before taxes…

radio.label: QD23A
radio.title: COLLAPSED INCOME GROUPS
